item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I plan on using this in a game that involves the SCP Foundation fighting against the Chaos Insurgency. I plan on having this be a mid-game to late-game item. It will also drain the player's HP while equipped. Any other suggestions for making sure it is not too OP (overpowered)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The execution isn't the best, but i really like the idea. It's one of those objects i "fantasized", for lack of a better word, as a kid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tend to agree with the majority of comments regarding this item - it's a nice idea for an SCP, as some of them need to be small things with anomalous properties that aren't just trying to kill all humans. However, the specific execution in this piece is definitely sub-par. Tighten up the writing style, chop out the gratuitous 'magic healing ray' tests and the reference to 682, and there's definite potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tone on this one's just…awful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I though of a sheet of paper pre-filled as testament

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Testing is suggested for SCP-682.

SCP-117 will produce this tool.

Also, people above me mention a rewrite, may I try to do it? I want to fix the writing, remove the notes, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with you. As it stands this SCP is one of those "It sure does exist and does a thing." I believe that this needs a rewrite. I believe this is from the old site and even if the person who posted this is the writer then they have been gone since 2012. Also a testing log would be fun to have. I don't know who would rewrite it (I can't because I am a very bad judge of what is good and bad in writing.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honest to god, I think this article could be a fantastic excuse for a crosstesting log. It addresses problems, and the Foundation has a lot of problems. In its current form though, it could also use a big overhaul for tone and structure, plus less expungement (I like some of it though).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A charming idea, I like the little twist that it sucks nutrients out of you to create tools. However, this article is really bizarrely phrased at certain parts and difficult to read? I'll keep it neutral for this one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All other tools always seem to be present, though, after the task is completed and the tool closed, unless faced with a task requiring that tool again, the tool cannot be found on the tool again.

Can SCP-117 make a thesaurus? -1 for being unreadable. The idea is cute, but not grabbing me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: too much expunged data seems like lazy writing :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, the process of becoming jaded to lolFoundation…
Brings back memories. X3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What complete fucking idiot would let any SCP anywhere near this thing? Seriously? Does it need explaining that a magic tool for whatever job needs doing could aid containment breech?

Other than that — not interesting, this kind of thing can't be interesting without a story or some kind of reaction.

item.title: Can it's healing beam heal the metal deficiency?
item.content:encoded:
Aside from the metal sucking downside; this is pretty much a deux-ex-machina for just about any otherwise unbeatable SCP.

At the very least it should be shown that the net effect of the healing is always doing more damage than it heals. Like for example, perhaps first have it perform some task that will consume an specific amount of metal (some chemical reaction or whatever, that will use up metal from the tool so it doesn't go back to the wielder after the use) twice, confirm that the 2 D-classes have a similar amount of metal deficiency and tell them to use 117 to heal each other, with the result being the last one to use it ends up with even less metal on his body after healing the other that is now back at normal levels of metal on his body. And perhaps a couple examples of attempts of termination of unbeatables like what sora2455 suggested above, where it always needs way more metal than any human body can provide to even start building the tool; allowing 117 to still appear to be limitless while actually imposing some limits to it's use in practice.

And I guess it might also be interesting to show what happens if it's attempted to be used on a corpse of someone killed by it's effects (perhaps the corpse achieves the metal levels up to almost what is expected on a living person, but the wielder dies in the process and the corpse stays dead?).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Testing is suggested for SCP-682.

"D-Class with SCP-117 entered 682 enclosure. SCP-117 begins to produce a tool far larger than any tool produced so far - several meters in length at least. However, before even the frame of the tool can be finished, the D-Class expired, having had all metal absorbed into 117.

On the bright side, 117 thought it was possible to kill 682. Just really hard. - Dr Vauge

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree it's a good item with tonal issues. No vote. What bugged me the most were the magical healing beams and the very thought of anyone allowing a magic item that gives you any tool you need within 10K of 682 on purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that this should be thaumiel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As much as

[DATA EXPUNGED] - A screwdriver.

made me laugh, I have to jump on the downvote bandwagon, for the lacklustre execution/writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I get what people see in it and understand that it's a relic from the old site, I don't think this is even close to the quality standards today and it really should really be rewritten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rarely downvote, but this managed to hit it for me. As interesting as the concept may be (and I sometimes even upvote interesting concepts), the execution here is very far off.
Edit: Apparently people browse recent comments or something. I should check the whole mainlist lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Generic magical item coupled with pointless SCP-682 reference.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can just imagine, that if tested on 682, it would either dispense a white flag, or tabasco sauce.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too much [DATA EXPUNGED] and too much cross-references.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looking at it, I like the idea, but I think it is in dire need of some touch-ups, both aesthetically and content wise.

There's some weird phrasing in some places, a few spots that toe the edge of proper tone, a single sentence addendum that just says "look at the thing under this," the Situation-Tool Produced log could probably use a proper table, two needles cross-references, the numerous notes at the end don't add too much, etc. etc.

I won't downvote it, but I'm hesitant to upvote in its current state.

EDIT: Also the location of recovery is clearly Tallahassee, Florida. I don't think the blackbox serves much of a purpose if you can determine what's under it that easily.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Please take the time to read, rate and comment this article, it should be given more attention

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Requesting permission from original author or person-of-authority to spruce up the clinical tone and formatting of this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like it better if it somehow retained the metals it had already taken, so it wouldn't just suck them out of your body every time you used it, only when it was deficient in a metal, so to speak. Because as it stands, it just seems like an item what's useful no wait its pointlessly harmful too.

I would like it better still if this had tone that wasn't awful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed w/ Scantron. I like this concept, but it really needs some tightening up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… like this in a weird way I guess? A tool that does anything by draining away the metals from your body interests me in a way I can't put my finger on. I can't upvote it because massive tone issues, but I wanna see something like this done better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well this isn't too useful or OTT at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this still a thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Totally ask him about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, this is vaguely unsettling, especially with the results on healthy people being good old [DATA EXPUNGED]. But still, Keter? Really, now. Keep it in a drawer, and it's Safe. It may be sentient, and might desire to be useful for that, but it's not like it can leave its case and come and get you.

Just don't have it within a hundred miles of the Broken God, and think carefully before showing it to SCP-914. 343 only knows what the Very Fine setting would do to it.
